method_summary: The study uses machine learning models (WATCH and TRIGGER) trained on Fermi-LAT light curves with 42 variability features from 365-day trailing windows to predict gamma-ray blazar flares within 90 days (WATCH) and 45 days (TRIGGER).
key_innovation: A strictly causal machine learning backtest approach that avoids temporal leakage by using only pre-cutoff data before MJD 60000 for all scaling, calibration, threshold selection, and validation.
datasets_used: Fermi-LAT light curves for 13 bright blazars
limitations_stated: No reliable pre-onset TRIGGER alert is obtained
title: Advance warning of $\gamma$-ray blazar flares from \textit{Fermi}-LAT light curves: a strictly causal machine-learning backtest
